subject: Geology
title: How Minerals Form
description: A micro-lesson that makes How Minerals Form usable.

hook.text: Some minerals crystallize from red-hot magma. Others grow slowly from evaporating seawater over centuries.

buildup.text: Minerals form through cooling magma, precipitation from water, pressure changes, or biological activity.

discovery.text: Slow cooling deep underground makes large crystals. Fast cooling at the surface makes tiny, almost invisible ones.

twist.text: The same chemical recipe can produce different minerals depending on temperature, pressure, and cooling speed.

climax.text: Mineral formation is controlled by physics and chemistry. Every crystal records the conditions of its birth.

punchline.text: Every crystal is a frozen moment in time.

quiz.question: What happens when magma cools slowly underground?
